news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: Evidence is mixed on how sentiment influences activity, but contacts in the midwest say confidence is changing how they behave.
Fed's Goolsbee: Uncertainty could feed into company capital plans, also sap confidence.
Fed's Goolsbee: The hard data right now are not the stagflation of the 1970s, but it is uncomfortable when inflation and unemployment are both moving higher.
Fed's Goolsbee: The response to stagflation would depend on a number of factors, but expectations would be very important.
Fed's Goolsbee: Responding to stagflation depends on the hit to inflation and jobs.
Fed's Goolsbee: Tariffs raise prices and reduce output, which is a stagflationary impulse.
Fed's Goolsbee: There is nothing more uncomfortable than a stagflationary environment; there is no generic answer to it.
Fed's Goolsbee: The Fed commitment to 2% inflation is rock solid.
Fed's Goolsbee: If market expectations of long run inflation start rising the Fed would have to act.
YXT.com Group Holding Limited to release 2024 financial results on March 27, 2025. Leader in digital learning in China.
news.summary: A leading company in the digital corporate learning industry in China, YXT.com Group Holding Limited (NASDAQ: YXT), based in Suzhou, revealed its intention to disclose its financial performance for the entire year ending on December 31, 2024. The information is set to be released after the closure of the U.S. market on March 27, 2025.

news.headline: Real Estate Split Corp. announces March 2025 distributions for Class A & Preferred shares. TSX: RS & RS.PR.A.
news.summary: Real Estate Split Corp, a company listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the symbols TSX: RS and RS.PR.A, has disclosed that a distribution for March 2025 will be issued to Class A shareholders. The specific amount of the distribution has not been revealed, however, the company confirmed that shareholders can expect to receive payments according to predetermined criteria.

news.headline: GIG SA approach for Company shares withdrawn, new proposal to acquire GIG Services SA; supported by majority shareholder.
news.summary: After deliberations on the GIG SA approach, the board has received advice that the previously mentioned offer for the Company's shares made on December 17, 2024, will not proceed. A new proposal has emerged to purchase GIG Services SA, an indirect subsidiary of the Company, from board members at GIG Services SA. This proposal is backed by entities associated with the primary beneficial shareholder in the Company.

news.headline: E Split Corp. (TSX: ENS, ENS.PR.A) to pay distribution to Class A shareholders for March 2025.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, E Split Corp. (TSX: ENS and ENS.PR.A) shared news about its upcoming distribution for March 2025. The Fund stated that the distribution will be payable to Class A shareholders.

news.headline: Star Bulk announced availability of its 2024 Annual Report on Form 20‐F, including audited financial statements filed on March 19, 2025.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, in Athens, Greece, Star Bulk Carriers Corp. shared news that its Annual Report on Form 20‐F, featuring audited financial statements for the fiscal year concluding on December 31, 2024, has been submitted to the Securities and Exchange Commission. This was done on March 19, 2025. People can access the Annual Report on the SEC's website at http://www.sec.gov and Star Bulk’s website at http://www.starbulk.com. The Company is listed on Nasdaq under the ticker symbol SBLK.

news.headline: DRYWORLD launches campaign for new compression line in partnership with New Zealand Super Rugby.
news.summary: DRYWORLD has initiated a new marketing campaign to introduce its latest graduated compression line. This launch comes in an official partnership with New Zealand Super Rugby. The campaign aims to highlight the innovative features of the compression line and promote its benefits to athletes. This strategic collaboration with Super Rugby allows DRYWORLD to reach a larger audience, with over 1.2 million fans on average tuning in to watch each game. The partnership provides a platform for DRYWORLD to showcase its products to a wide demographic of sports enthusiasts and professionals.

news.headline: TOMI partners with Algafeed for aquaculture; introduces SteraMist technology.
news.summary: In Frederick, Maryland on March 21, 2025, TOMI Environmental Solutions, Inc.® (NASDAQ: TOMZ), known for disinfection and decontamination solutions, revealed the effective incorporation of its SteraMist technology at Algafeed. Algafeed, a leading enterprise in large-scale microalgae cultivation for aquaculture and fish nourishment, has adopted TOMI's innovative technology for their operations.

news.headline: Nano Dimension (Nasdaq: NNDM) will release its 2024 Annual Report in April 2025. No specific date mentioned.
news.summary: In Waltham, Massachusetts, on March 21, 2025, Nano Dimension Ltd. (Nasdaq: NNDM), a leading provider of digital manufacturing solutions, revealed plans to unveil its 2024 Annual Report in April 2025.

news.headline: BioCardia will report 2024 financial results on March 26 and host a conference call on March 31, 2025.
news.summary: In Sunnyvale, California, on March 21, 2025, BioCardia®, Inc. [NASDAQ:BCDA], a company specializing in cellular and cell-derived therapeutics for cardiovascular and pulmonary conditions, revealed plans to disclose its financial outcomes for the year closing on December 31, 2024 on Wednesday, March 26, 2025. Additionally, it will conduct a corporate update conference call on Monday, March 31, 2025, with specific details on time and dial-in information to be provided later. This announcement comes following the presentation of the results from the CardiAMP-HF Trial titled, “A Double-blind, Randomized Controlled Trial of an Autologous Cell Therapy in Patients with HFrEF: Principal Results,” during the American College of Cardiology 2025 Scientific Sessions in Chicago on March 30, 2025.

news.headline: Xcel Brands to implement a 1-for-10 reverse stock split on March 25, 2025.
news.summary: On March 25, 2025, a 1-for-10 reverse stock split will be implemented at the opening of trading. This means that for every 10 shares an investor owns, they will receive 1 share following the split.

news.headline: West Bancorporation, Inc. (Nasdaq: WTBA) to announce Q1 results on April 24, 2025 before markets open.
news.summary: West Bancorporation, Inc., the parent company of West Bank, based in West Des Moines, Iowa, is set to announce its first quarter results for 2025 on Thursday, April 24, 2025, before the markets open. The company, listed on Nasdaq as WTBA, will provide insights into its financial performance during this period. Investors and stakeholders are keenly waiting to analyze the figures and outlook presented by the Company. This update marks a significant event for those following the financial activities of West Bancorporation, Inc., as they await the disclosed numbers which will offer an indication of the company's standing and trajectory in the market.

news.headline: Neon Bloom partnering with Genwin LLC for quicker E-commerce & retail payment processing, enhancing efficiency.
news.summary: Neon Bloom has announced a strategic partnership with Genwin LLC aimed at enhancing e-commerce and retail payment transaction processing. With this collaboration, Neon Bloom aims to expedite payment transactions for online and in-store retail purchases. This initiative will significantly boost the speed and efficiency of processing transactions, leading to quicker and smoother e-commerce experiences. The partnership is set to improve transaction speeds by up to 40%, benefitting both online retailers and customers alike.

news.headline: FibroBiologics to present at German-Nordic ECM Meeting with 160+ patents, aims to treat chronic diseases using fibroblasts.
news.summary: Houston-based FibroBiologics, Inc. (Nasdaq: FBLG) revealed that they will have their Chief Scientific Officer, Hamid Khoja, Ph.D., discuss the company's innovative fibroblast cell-based strategy for treating chronic illnesses at The German-Nordic Joint Extracellular Matrix Meeting in Freiburg, Germany from March 26-28, 2025. FibroBiologics, a clinical-stage biotech firm, holds over 160 patents and is dedicated to developing therapies and potential cures for chronic diseases utilizing fibroblasts and fibroblast-derived materials. The event serves as a yearly platform to highlight advancements in extracellular matrix biology.

news.headline: Runway Growth extends credit facility with KeyBank and expands Board of Directors from 5 to 8 members.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, in Menlo Park, California, Runway Growth Finance Corp. (Nasdaq: RWAY) announced operational updates, including amending and extending its credit agreement with KeyBank National Association on March 18, 2025. The company, a major provider of flexible capital solutions for late- and growth-stage firms looking for alternatives to equity fundraising, also increased its Board size from five to eight members.

news.headline: VERSES® partners with AI Driller for smarter oil and gas ops. Genius™ predictive tool to enhance safety and efficiency.
news.summary: Genius™️ is revolutionizing the oil and gas industry with its advanced predictive capabilities that are set to enhance safety and efficiency in drilling operations. By harnessing cutting-edge technology, Genius™️ is paving the way for smarter solutions that promise to optimize processes and mitigate risks. This innovation comes at a crucial time when the industry is under increasing pressure to prioritize safety and sustainability. With a projected reduction of up to 30% in drilling incidents and a potential increase of 20% in operational efficiency, the impact of Genius™️ is expected to be significant. The integration of these predictive capabilities is poised to bring about a transformation in the sector, setting new standards for best practices in drilling operations.

news.headline: Infrastructure Dividend Split Corp. (TSX:IS) announces March 2025 distribution for Class A shareholders.
news.summary: On March 21, 2025, Infrastructure Dividend Split Corp. (TSX:IS) revealed that a distribution for March 2025 will be granted to Class A shareholders.

news.headline: Maris-Tech to present advanced AI video solutions at MDEX 2025 at SIBAT Booth F3 for 360° situational awareness.
news.summary: Experience the latest advancements in tactical video and AI solutions by visiting the Maris-Tech team at Booth F3 during the SIBAT event. Explore groundbreaking technology with a team dedicated to innovation and excellence.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: A slowdown in the economy would be a reason to cut, but if inflation rises beyond the tariffs, or hits expectations, the Fed would have to revise the outlook.
KB Home expected to report earnings of $1.58/share, revenue $1.50B in Q1; Dividend yield 1.63% annually. Wells Fargo lowers price target to $63.
news.summary: Homebuilder KB Home (NYSE:KBH) is set to announce its first-quarter financial results on Monday, March 24. Analysts anticipate earnings of $1.58 per share for the quarter, lower than the $1.76 per share reported in the same period last year. The company is projected to reveal quarterly revenue of $1.50 billion, up from $1.47 billion year-over-year. Wells Fargo analyst Sam Reid maintained an Underweight rating on KB Home and decreased the price target from $65 to $63 on March 10. Additionally, KB Home offers an annual dividend yield of 1.63%, with a quarterly dividend of 25 cents per share.

news.headline: Planet Labs Stock drops after missing Q4 earnings: Adj losses of 8 cents/share, revenue $61.6m, EoP 976 customers, backlog $498.5m, non-GAAP gross margin 65%. adj EBITDA profit $2.4m.
news.summary: Planet Labs PBC (NYSE:PL) saw a decrease in premarket trading on Friday following its fourth-quarter earnings report. The company reported adjusted losses of 8 cents per share, missing the expected 2 cents. Despite revenue reaching $61.6 million, a 5% increase from the previous year, it fell short of the anticipated $61.87 million. The fourth quarter saw 97% of Recurring Annual Contract Value (ACV) and 976 End of Period (EoP) Customer Count. As of January 31, 2025, the backlog amounted to $498.5 million. Non-GAAP gross margin improved to 65% from 58% in the same quarter of the prior year. Adjusted EBITDA saw a positive turn, with a profit of $2.4 million compared to a loss of $9.8 million in the fourth quarter of fiscal year 2024.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: The longer the Fed waits it may mean the cuts have to be backloaded, there can be a cost to waiting.
Fed's Goolsbee: Beyond tariffs the Fed also has to think about coming tax cuts and other issues.
Canadian pension fund reconsidering private equity strategy due to global risks.
Fed's Goolsbee: I still believe the economy is resilient and if there is progress on inflation rates will be lower in 12 to 18 months.
Fed's Goolsbee: When there is uncertainty it is a time to get as much data as possible.
Interest in Mana Wine's bonded warehouse rises due to impending 200% tariff, offering secure storage and tax deferral for foreign alcohol imports.
news.summary: In Jersey City, New Jersey, importers are seeking refuge at Mana Wine's bonded warehouse as they face a staggering 200% tariff on foreign alcohol imports. This facility provides a secure storage solution for imported goods and offers tax deferral benefits to help navigate the financial impact of the tariff. This move comes amidst rising tensions in the trade industry and concerns over escalating tariffs.

news.headline: Namibia inaugurates first female President amid impending oil boom.

Fed's Goolsbee: We need to be careful when you say transitory, the answer to what "transitory" means this time around is whether the tariffs apply to intermediate goods, stoke retaliation, and other factors.
Fed's Goolsbee: Imports are only 11% of GDP, so one time tariffs that are not followed by retaliation are more likely to be transitory.
Fed's Goolsbee: The bigger they are and the more like supply shocks they are the harder it will be for the Fed to look through them.
Fed's Goolsbee: Before judging how monetary policy reacts to tariffs, the Fed needs to know how long the tariffs last, possible retaliation, pass through to consumers.
Canada February retail sales expected to be down 0.4% - Statscan Flash Estimate.
Fed's Goolsbee: There's a lot of strength in the hard economic data.
Fed's Goolsbee: Unemployment and inflation do reflect progress towards the dual mandate.
Bitcoin is a strong buy now, says Bitwise CIO Matt Hougan, calling it the best time in history due to political developments.
news.summary: Matt Hougan, Chief Investment Officer at Bitwise, believes Bitcoin (BTC) is primed for the future due to various political factors. During the Blockworks Digital Asset Summit in New York, he highlighted that current macroeconomic conditions are strengthening Bitcoin's long-term investment outlook but exerting short-term price pressures. Hougan stated that this is the most optimal time in history, considering risk factors, to purchase Bitcoin.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: Current conditions are maybe a shock to the economy depending on how long they last.
Fed's Goolsbee: There is still a lot of strength in the economy right now.
Fed's Goolsbee: Markets want information fast but that is not realistic at this moment.
Accelerate Diagnostics reports Q4 net sales of $2.8 million, gross margin at 16%. FY net sales at $11.7 million with $50.0 million net loss.
news.summary: Accelerate Diagnostics, Inc. reported their financial results for the fourth quarter and full year ended December 31, 2024. In the fourth quarter, net sales were $2.8 million, with a net loss of $9.6 million, resulting in a $0.38 net loss per share. They ended the quarter with $16.3 million in cash and cash equivalents, down from $20.9 million at the start. For the full year, net sales were $11.7 million, with a gross margin of 23% and a net loss of $50.0 million, equating to a $2.15 net loss per share. SG&A costs were $21.3 million for the year, down from $31.2 million in the prior year. R&D costs were $16.7 million for the year, lower than the $25.4 million in the prior year. The company plans not to host regular earnings conference calls but will provide financial updates through SEC filings and investor relations materials on their website. The full financial results for 2024 will be filed with the SEC. Non-GAAP financial measures have been used in this press release to evaluate operating performance and trends.

news.headline: Fed's Goolsbee: When you have a lot of uncertainty you have to wait for things to clear up.
Fed's Goolsbee: Businesses have anxiety, waiting on capex re tariffs.
